text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Amelia Grant  
City University Hospital  
Department of Neurology, 12 Riverbank Way, Manchester M3 4QH  

Dear Dr Amelia Grant,  

Michael Davies, DOB: 14-02-1991, Hospital No: M983472 NHS No. 6045198723  
Flat 2B, 17 Copper Street, Manchester, M15 6AL 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent episodes suggestive of focal-onset seizures with impaired awareness. MRI brain (July 2025) normal. Routine EEG (August 2025) unremarkable. 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 500 mg bd 

Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily.  
- Provide written workplace safety advice regarding prolonged standing, heat, and crowd surges; patient to share with employer’s occupational health.  
- Maintain a regular sleep schedule and adequate hydration.  
- Report any escalation in event frequency or occurrence with loss of consciousness or injury.  
- Seizure diary to continue; follow-up in 6 months, sooner if concerns.  

I reviewed Michael Davies in clinic today. He works on the gate line at a busy metro station where prolonged standing, variable temperatures, and occasional crowd surges are routine. Over the last two months he has documented five to six focal automatisms during the last two months, typically brief (30–60 seconds) episodes of lip-smacking and right-hand picking movements, followed by several minutes of confusion and fatigue. He reports that missed meals, poor sleep after late shifts, and sensory overload at peak times may precede events. There has been no tongue biting, no witnessed generalised convulsions, and no injuries. 

He commenced Levetiracetam 500 mg twice a day in early August 2025, with initial irritability that settled after two weeks; no other adverse effects reported. Bloods including FBC, U&E and LFTs in September 2025 were within normal ranges. MRI brain and routine EEG have not identified a structural or electrophysiological correlate. 

Given the persisting brief episodes, I advised continued adherence to medication and practical risk reduction at work: avoiding solo posts during the busiest periods where feasible, ensuring access to water, and arranging short rotation breaks to mitigate prolonged standing and heat. He will continue his seizure diary and has been advised to inform a trusted colleague of his typical events and recovery period. Driving advice has been given in line with current DVLA regulations for seizures with impaired awareness; he understands the requirement to cease driving and to notify the DVLA if applicable. 

I will review him in six months, or earlier if there is any deterioration, emergence of convulsive seizures, or injury at work. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: Over the last two months he has documented five to six focal automatisms during the last two months
five to six focal automatisms during the last two months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "Over the last two months he has documented five to six focal automatisms during the last two months." This provides a clear numeric range and timeframe. No other frequencies are given that are higher. Therefore, normalize to the allowed format as a range per 2 months.
